title: Operational Affordance Planning Surface Review
goal: Catch planning fields whose allowed values or routing obligations are not obvious before validation, using the closeout/status/durable-residue friction as the bounded review prompt.
scope: planning execplan schema and archive validation code that define the actual allowed values
non_goals: Promote findings into active planning automatically.
Review unrelated planning, memory, or external-work behavior.

review_mode.review question: Do the current planning authoring surfaces make closeout status and durable residue routing obvious enough that agents do not have to learn allowed values from validation failures?
review_mode.inputs inspected first: AGENTS.md, .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml, .agentic-workspace/planning/reviews/README.md, docs/operational-affordance-design.md, execplan template/schema, planning checker, and archive-plan validation code

review_method.commands used: uv run agentic-workspace start --format json; uv run agentic-workspace skills --target . --task "bounded review or finding capture for operational affordance ambiguous fields" --format json; uv run agentic-workspace report --target . --format json; rg over status/residue/closure fields; targeted Get-Content reads of templates, schemas, checker, and installer validation code

findings.title: Closed planning state has a hidden value vocabulary
findings.summary: The state validator requires closed items to use `maturity = "closed"`, `status = "done"` or `"dismissed"`, and explicit durable-residue/closure routing, but the raw `state.toml` surface and compact process docs do not expose a copyable closed-item scaffold.
findings.evidence: The runtime constants define planning-state statuses as `deferred`, `next`, `active`, `blocked`, `done`, and `dismissed`, and closed-item validation then requires `status` to be `done` or `dismissed` plus residue routing. The current live state has roadmap rows but no checked-in closed-row template adjacent to the state file, so a hand edit can plausibly use natural wording like `closed` until validation rejects it.
findings.risk if unchanged: Agents will continue to discover closed-state wording by failing validation, which is exactly the operational-affordance failure described in docs/operational-affordance-design.md.
findings.suggested action: Add a compact writer/scaffold path for closed planning-state rows, or expose allowed values and a valid example in the summary/doctor/action output that points agents away from raw hand-authored closure rows.
findings.promotion target: .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml roadmap candidate, likely under the existing dogfooding-closeout-friction lane rather than a new active item
findings.promotion trigger: Promote when closeout/state validation friction recurs or when the existing closeout-friction lane is selected for implementation.

findings.title: Execplan closeout template hides required terminal values
findings.summary: The canonical execplan template leaves `closure_check` values blank even though archive validation later requires narrow values for slice status, larger-intent status, closure decision, intent satisfaction, and routed continuation.
findings.evidence: The template has blank `slice status`, `larger-intent status`, and `closure decision` fields, while archive validation requires `slice status` to be `complete`, `completed`, or `bounded slice complete`; `closure decision` to be `archive-and-close` or `archive-but-keep-lane-open`; and larger-intent status to match the selected closure path.
findings.risk if unchanged: Agents that correctly open the template can still author invalid or semantically weak closeout text because the allowed terminal states are only apparent in prose docs or after archive validation.
findings.suggested action: Make closeout valid-by-construction: either change the template to include allowed-value hints or examples, or make `archive-plan --prepare-closeout` the primary closeout action object surfaced by summary/preflight before agents edit closeout fields directly.
findings.promotion target: .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml roadmap candidate under dogfooding-closeout-friction or a narrow planning-surface affordance lane
findings.promotion trigger: Promote when the next closeout-affordance slice is selected or if another archive/summary validator failure cites closeout wording.

findings.title: Durable residue status disagrees between schema and archive validation
findings.summary: `planning-execplan.schema.json` permits `durable_residue.status = "pending"`, but archive validation rejects `pending` and reports the allowed values without it.
findings.evidence: The schema enum includes `pending` for `durable_residue.status`, while `EXECPLAN_DURABLE_RESIDUE_STATUSES` omits `pending` and `_invalid_durable_residue_message` rejects any status outside `none`, `memory`, `docs`, `contract`, `check`, `planning`, and `evidence_only` before archive.
findings.risk if unchanged: A record can look schema-valid during normal record validation but still fail the lifecycle operation that matters for closeout, teaching agents a contradictory contract.
findings.suggested action: Align schema, template, and archive validation on one durable-residue lifecycle vocabulary. If `pending` is only an active-plan placeholder, model that explicitly outside the terminal archive contract or have `--prepare-closeout` normalize it before validation.
findings.promotion trigger: Promote before the next schema or archive-plan closeout change, or immediately if this mismatch is causing current validation failures.

recommendation.promote: Promote the durable-residue schema mismatch first if a narrow fix is desired; it is the most deterministic defect.
recommendation.defer: Defer broader state/closeout affordance improvements to the existing dogfooding-closeout-friction lane unless validation friction recurs.
recommendation.dismiss: No finding should become an active TODO item from this review alone.

retention.trigger: After schema/template/writer-helper fixes land or the findings are promoted into roadmap state.
retention.proof surface: planning schema, archive-plan tests, and a valid-by-construction closeout/state helper or surfaced action object
retention.source retention rule: Store decision, confidence, source refs, and reopen/promote triggers; do not copy issue titles, current status snapshots, long command output, or source summaries unless the copied text is the judgment itself.
retention.distillation path: When decisions are promoted into issues, planning state, Memory, docs, contracts, or checks, keep this artifact as evidence/history and remove or summarize reconstructable source context at the next cleanup.

review_finding.rule: Use only these headings when a prose finding is needed; keep the canonical fields structured.

field_map.Next owner: execution_summary/follow-on routed to or required_continuation/owner surface

handoff_or_closeout.rule: Prefer structured fields; use this shape only for short human-readable summaries.

drift_log: 2026-04-30: Review created from closeout/status/residue validation friction and operational-affordance criteria.
